data.title: Wall Street notches record close after soft CPI data, posts best week since early August
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday soared and ended at a record high after market participants, starved of economic data during the ongoing U.S. government shutdown, received a reprieve in the form of a cooler-than-expected consumer inflation report.

All three major averages notched record closes, with the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) earlier surpassing the 6,800 points level for the first time ever. The gauge eventually concluded +0.8%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished +1% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced +1.2%. 

Before the opening bell, data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics showed headline CPI coming in at +0.3% M/M, versus an expected figure of +0.4%. Core CPI for September was +0.2% M/M, versus a consensus of +0.3%. On a Y/Y basis, core CPI was +3.0%, still well above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. 

The CPI print reinforced expectations that the Fed would deliver another 25 basis point interest rate cut at its monetary policy committee meeting next week. Meanwhile, the data could be the last inflation indicator for a while, as the White House said the October CPI report likely won't be released.

"Today’s CPI is consistent with a +0.2% MoM reading in the more important core PCE deflator (which will be delayed). That would make the third such tame reading in a row and the fifth in the past six months. The light is getting greener for the Fed to cut next week and keep on going until the 3% neutral Fed funds estimate, at the very least," David Rosenberg, founder and president of Rosenberg Research & Associates, said on X (formerly Twitter).

Friday's session also capped a strong five days for markets, with the S&P 500's (SP500) +1.9% gain its best weekly performance since early August. The Dow (DJI) was +2.2% for the week, while the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was +2.3%. 

“The market this week did what the market loves to do, which is some combination of trying to bore (the S&P) or depress (the Nasdaq) investors into deciding the top is in and to get out; whereupon the inevitable pop comes Friday," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.  

"With all three major indices at all-time highs, this market remains a powerful bull. It has also been a very tricky market all year – many highly skilled investors have not kept pace with the S&P; the simple buy-the-dip reflex has been the best strategy all year. We continue to look higher into year-end,” King added.          

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were largely lower across the board. They had initially slid as investors had snapped up bonds after the CPI report. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 1 basis point to 3.99%, while the shorter-end, more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.48%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: BLK gained after six consecutive sessions of losses
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) gained after six consecutive sessions of losses, as the company’s shares closed 1.25% higher at $1139.01 on Friday.

In the preceding six sessions, the company lost over 3.96%, compared to a 1.65% rise in the S&P 500 Index. Furthermore, in YTD 2025, shares of the company have gone down by over 11.04% compared to a gain of 14.57% in the broader S&P 500 Index.

BLK Q3 2025 revenue and earnings exceeded the average analyst estimate as total net inflows reached a record, helping to drive 10% organic base fee growth.

The company's revenue in the third quarter grew to $6.51B from $5.42B in Q2 2025 and $5.20B in Q3 2024.

The company's Q3 2025 adjusted EPS of $11.55 topped the average analyst estimate of $11.33, slipped from $12.05 in Q2 2025, and increased from $11.46 in Q3 2024.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, BLK has a Hold rating with a score of 3.12 out of 5. The company was rated A for profitability, while it got a B for growth and a B+ for momentum.

According to Wall Street analysts, 14 rated the stock a Buy or above, and 3 rated it a Hold.

Similarly, Seeking Alpha analysts appeared rather bullish about the stock, issuing a Buy call for BLK.

Seeking Alpha analyst Motti Sapir rated it a Buy, reporting that BLK remains a long-term buy, with recent acquisitions and expansion into technology, infrastructure, and real estate supporting future growth. The company's unique combination of index funds, private markets, and technology creates a competitive edge that rivals struggle to match.

data.title: Invesco postpones proxy vote on QQQ fund conversion
data.description:
Inesco (NYSE:IVZ) has delayed a key vote on the converting of its QQQ unit investment trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) into an open-ended exchange-traded fund, adjourning the shareholder meeting to Dec. 5, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

The plan to change the $400B tech fund's structure required participation from over half of all voting shareholders.

Invesco (IVZ) could postponse the meeting if the quorum requirement wasn't met, it has said in its proxy statement.

IVZ shares were on track to close Friday's session up 1.1%, after tumbling as much as 9% earlier in the session.

data.title: Jacobs Solutions seesaws amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Jacobs Solutions (NYSE:J) seesaws amid takeover speculation for the engineering and infrastucture firm.
  • Jacobs (J) is a target for WSP Global (TSX:WSP:CA), according to a  StreetInsider report on Friday, which cited one unidentified source. Jacobs hired investment bank Centerview Partners to review the bid from WSP.The offer has both a cash and stock component, costing of mainly stock.
  • The offer may not lead to a sale, according to the report. WSP Global and Jacobs didn't respond ro StreetInsider's request for comments.

data.title: Top ETFs with heavy exposure to Magnificent 7 stocks as big tech week lies ahead
data.description:
With Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) having reported its Q3 earnings this week, Wall Street is eagerly anticipating quarterly results from the remaining players in the Magnificent 7 club, which include Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Meta (NASDAQ:META), and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), set to be revealed in the week ahead.

ETFs with significant exposure to these companies are preparing for the potential impact of their quarterly reports, as investors look forward to updates regarding their AI initiatives.

Here are several ETFs with some of the largest exposure to the Magnificent 7 stocks:

  • Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (BATS:MAGS) - This fund directly invests in the Magnificent 7 stocks using an equal-weighted strategy.
  • MicroSectors FANG+ ETN (NYSEARCA:FNGS) - The NYSE FANG+ Index, which underlies this ETN, is an equal-dollar weighted index designed to provide exposure to a selection of highly-traded growth stocks from next-generation technology and tech-enabled firms.
  • Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:MGK) - With more than half of its portfolio allocated to the seven mega-cap tech giants, this ETF offers broad exposure to large-cap growth stocks, including those in the Magnificent 7.
  • Invesco S&P 500 Top 50 ETF (NYSEARCA:XLG) - This ETF tracks the S&P 500 Top 50 Index, which consists of the 50 largest companies within the S&P 500 Index.
  • Roundhill Daily 2X Long Magnificent Seven ETF (BATS:MAGX) - This ETF does not hold the Magnificent Seven stocks directly, but invests in swaps and the standard Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF to achieve twice the daily performance of the Magnificent Seven stocks. 
  • Direxion Daily Magnificent 7 Bull 2X Shares ETF (NYSEARCA:QQQU) - This ETF seeks to track 2x the daily performance of the Indxx Magnificent 7 Index and is equally weighted among the seven companies.
  • Direxion Daily Magnificent 7 Bear 1X Shares ETF (NYSEARCA:QQQD) - This ETF is structured to have an inverse relationship with the Magnificent 7 stocks, aiming to deliver the opposite daily performance of the Indxx Magnificent 7 Index.

The final company in the Magnificent 7 to report its earnings will be Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), with its announcement expected in November.
